title: hypericum armenum subsp. iranicum
intro: Hypericum armenum subsp. iranicum, also known as the Iranian St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin infections. The health benefits of Hypericum armenum subsp. iranicum include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression, promote wound healing, and improve sleep quality, making it a popular herbal remedy in Middle Eastern and European folk medicine. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
